summary: First end-to-end test of Jarvis for Home (WhatsApp-style household chat + Claude Sonnet 4.5 + mock Swiggy). Backend: 26/26 pytest passing (100%). Frontend: critical UX flows verified — landing → register (create-a-home tab) → chat page with header/badge, household dialog with invite code + rotate, real Claude LLM replies to Jarvis-tagged questions while staying silent on casual chat, silent grocery extraction into cart, auto cart_proposal card after >=4 items, Cart page with clear MOCKED Swiggy banner, manual add + checkout, sidebar nav across Cart/Overview/Meals/Pantry/Cook/Family, join-home flow via invite code, login flow. One minor playwright assertion intermittently failed due to shared backend state between reruns (cart row counts), not a product bug.

issues: Terracotta 'Order on Swiggy' button has low visibility in screenshots at quality=40, but is clickable and labelled. Consider stronger contrast or border so it doesn't blend into the white card on the right.
Radix DialogContent missing aria-describedby — console warnings logged. Non-blocking but a11y nit.

action_items: (Optional) Improve checkout button contrast/visibility on Cart page checkout-bar.
(Optional) Add aria-describedby (or DialogDescription) to members dialog to silence Radix accessibility warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1063 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, household, chat, cart, mealplan, ai) for maintainability.
Cart de-dup uses lowercase name only; 'milk' vs '2 litre milk' both treated as 'milk' which is fine, but 'curd' vs 'fresh curd' from chat might unintentionally collide. Acceptable for POC.
/api/chat/since uses string ISO comparison which works because timestamps are ISO-8601 with timezone — solid.

JARVIS_SYSTEM prompt is well-designed (silent vs reply rules, JSON-only output). LLM behaved correctly in tests (silent on 'hello everyone', extracted from 'we are out of toor dal', replied to 'Jarvis what should we cook tonight?').
Mocked Swiggy clearly marked: response field `mocked: true`, system message tagged `[MOCKED]`, UI banner explicit. Good transparency.
Auth: bcrypt + JWT correctly implemented; password_hash properly excluded from /household members response.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% — all critical flows verified end-to-end; one assertion failed due to test-state collision, not a product bug

test_credentials: p1@test.com / password (Priya) and r1@test.com / password (Rohan) from test_credentials.md still work. Test suite creates fresh TEST_priya_<runid>@test.com users each run.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates a unique household (TEST_Sharma_Home / TEST_UI_Sharma) and two users prefixed TEST_. No cleanup is performed — household data accumulates in MongoDB across runs but uses unique emails so no collision.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_jarvis_home.py covers all 26 critical paths including LLM-driven Jarvis reply/silence, grocery extraction, cart auto-proposal at >=4 items, and mock Swiggy checkout. LLM tests take ~20s due to real Claude calls. Reuse this file for regression. Frontend manual click-through verified: register-create / register-join / chat / cart / checkout / sidebar nav / login. No critical bugs found.
rca of the issue: N/A — no product bugs. The single Playwright cart-row assertion intermittence was caused by test re-runs sharing backend state; not a code issue.
